uploadZone.clickToUpload: Click to upload or drag and drop
uploadZone.supportedFormats: Supports audio (M4A, MP3, WAV) and video (MP4, MOV, AVI) formats
uploadZone.outcomeHint: After uploading, files are transcribed automatically into an editable transcript and subtitles (SRT), with speaker identification and AI punctuation.
uploadZone.filesTooLargeMessage: {count} file(s) exceed the {max}MB limit and were skipped

welcome.title: Welcome to SoundLite
welcome.desc: A 30-second tour from upload to viewing your results. Press ✕ to skip anytime.

upload.title: ① Upload audio
upload.desc: Click here or drag and drop an audio/video file. Supports MP3, M4A, WAV, MP4 and more.

language.title: ② Choose language
language.desc: Leave it on "Auto-detect" and we'll figure it out, or pick a language manually for better accuracy.

diarize.title: ③ Speaker identification
diarize.desc: Turn this on to automatically separate speakers; the transcript is labelled "SPEAKER_01, 02…".

taskType.title: ④ Output format
taskType.desc: "Document" produces readable paragraphs; "Subtitles" produces time-coded text segments, ideal for video captions.

start.title: ⑤ Start transcribing
start.desc: Click here once you're set. Let's use a sample task to show you what happens next →

list.title: ⑥ Your tasks
list.desc: Uploaded tasks show up here — you can watch progress while transcribing and open them once done. Click "Open transcript" to see the result →

transcript.title: ⑦ Transcript & speakers
transcript.desc: This is the transcription. With speaker identification on, speakers are labelled automatically — rename them by editing or find-and-replace.

actions.title: ⑧ Download & share
actions.desc: From this menu you can download the transcript (TXT, PDF) or subtitles (SRT, VTT), or create a share link for others to view without signing in.

summary.title: ⑨ AI summary
summary.desc: Generate an AI summary of the content in one click to grasp the key points without reading the whole transcript.

audio.title: ⑩ Follow the audio
audio.desc: The built-in player lets you read while listening — hold "Alt" and click any line to jump to its timestamp. Great for proofreading.
